tool_input.summary: The PDS catalog does not directly label Juno observations by Jupiter auroral zone (main oval vs polar cap vs satellite footprints), nor does it tag ‘H+/H3+ cyclotron waves’ as a distinct product. To determine where such ion-cyclotron/EMIC-like waves occur, you typically combine Juno/Waves wave spectra (and MAG background B to compute gyrofrequencies) with SPICE geometry/footprints and (optionally) UVS/JIRAM auroral context. Below are the relevant PDS datasets you can use to perform that zone-mapping analysis.

candidates.title: JNO E J SS WAV 2 EDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Primary Juno/Waves Level-2 EDR waveforms/spectra used to detect low-frequency ion cyclotron/EMIC wave power near ion gyrofrequencies during auroral passes.

candidates.title: JNO E J SS WAV 3 CDR BSTFULL V2.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno/Waves calibrated/derived (CDR) broadband/full-resolution product useful for surveying wave power across auroral traversals and identifying cyclotron-band features.

candidates.title: JNO E J SS WAV 3 CDR SRVFULL V2.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno/Waves calibrated/derived (CDR) survey/full product useful for statistical occurrence studies (e.g., where ion-cyclotron-band enhancements are most common vs latitude/MLT).

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 bundle providing electron densities (and related characteristic frequencies) derived from Waves—helpful context for interpreting wave modes and propagation regions when mapping occurrences to auroral zones.

candidates.title: JNO J 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated Juno magnetometer data needed to compute local ion gyrofrequencies (H+, heavier ions) to confirm cyclotron-wave identification and to relate wave occurrence to auroral field-line regions.

candidates.title: JNO SS 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Additional calibrated MAG/FGM dataset (spacecraft subsystem context) that can be paired with Waves for gyrofrequency and magnetic mapping during auroral crossings.

candidates.title: JUNO SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels required to map Juno position to magnetic footprints and to bin wave detections by auroral zone proxies (e.g., footprint latitude/MLT relative to the main oval/polar region).

candidates.title: ATM Volume jnouvs_2001 (JUNO UVS OBSERVATIONS)
candidates.reasoning: UVS auroral images/spectra provide contemporaneous auroral morphology (main oval, polar emissions) to classify the auroral zone corresponding to in-situ wave observations.

candidates.title: ATM Volume jnouvs_3001 (JUNO UVS OBSERVATIONS)
candidates.reasoning: Reduced/derived UVS products for easier comparison with in-situ Waves/MAG intervals when determining which auroral zone Juno is connected to.

candidates.title: Juno UVS Calibrated Data collection file
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 calibrated UVS collection; useful for combining auroral-zone context with in-situ wave detections in a modern PDS4 archive.

parts.args: {"summary":"The PDS catalog does not directly label Juno observations by Jupiter auroral zone (main oval vs polar cap vs satellite footprints), nor does it tag ‘H+/H3+ cyclotron waves’ as a distinct product. To determine where such ion-cyclotron/EMIC-like waves occur, you typically combine Juno/Waves wave spectra (and MAG background B to compute gyrofrequencies) with SPICE geometry/footprints and (optionally) UVS/JIRAM auroral context. Below are the relevant PDS datasets you can use to perform that zone-mapping analysis.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"JNO-E_J_SS-WAV-2-EDR-V1.0","title":"JNO E J SS WAV 2 EDR
